smells.title: High duplicate context ratio
smells.whatHappened: Roughly 31% of the input tokens were repeated content.
smells.whyItMatters: High duplication means the model is spending a large share of the context budget on information it has already seen.
smells.evidenceSummary: 351 duplicate tokens across repeated project instructions and file trees.
smells.recommendation: Deduplicate repeated instructions, file trees, and logs across turns to reclaim context budget.
suggestedNextSteps: Open the Context Timeline to see which blocks repeat across turns.
Identify the top repeated groups and remove or cache them.

smells.title: Generated artifact in context
smells.whatHappened: One block looks like generated or build output.
smells.whyItMatters: Generated files are usually not helpful for reasoning about source code and can consume significant budget.
smells.recommendation: Exclude generated directories and minified bundles from the context window.
suggestedNextSteps: Review your file-inclusion rules for generated directories.
Add dist, build, and coverage to an ignore list and re-analyze the run.

smells.title: Repeated project instruction
smells.whatHappened: The same project instruction (AGENTS.md) was sent 3 times.
smells.whyItMatters: Project instructions rarely change during a run. Resending them inflates the context window and crowds out task-specific information.
smells.recommendation: Move persistent instructions into a system prompt or cache them rather than repeating them every turn.
suggestedNextSteps: Compare the repeated blocks in the Context Timeline.
Check whether your agent framework supports a persistent project instruction.

smells.title: Repeated file tree
smells.whatHappened: The same file tree was sent 3 times.
smells.whyItMatters: File trees are often large and change infrequently during a run.
smells.recommendation: Send file-tree diffs or omit the tree when it has not changed since the previous turn.
suggestedNextSteps: Inspect the file-tree blocks to see how much is repeated.
Try sending only files that changed.

smells.title: Lockfile included in context
smells.whatHappened: A dependency lockfile was included in the context window.
smells.whyItMatters: Lockfiles can be large and are only useful when dependency resolution is the active task.
smells.recommendation: Only include lockfiles when dependency changes are being discussed; otherwise omit them.
suggestedNextSteps: Check whether the lockfile was included intentionally.
Remove it from the context and compare token usage.

smells.title: Likely relevant file appeared late
smells.whatHappened: A source file (src/app.ts) first appeared in turn 3, after the midpoint of the run.
smells.whyItMatters: Relevant source files that appear late may be crowded out by earlier noise.
smells.evidenceSummary: File src/app.ts appeared in turn 3 of 4.
smells.recommendation: Try to surface files most relevant to the current task earlier in the run.
suggestedNextSteps: Verify that the file is actually relevant to the task.
Check whether the agent could have included this file earlier.

recommendations.title: Deduplicate repeated context
recommendations.description: Review repeated context across turns and remove or cache duplicates to reclaim budget.
recommendations.whyItMatters: High duplication means a large share of the budget is spent on information the model has already seen.
recommendations.inspectSuggestion: Open the Context Timeline and sort blocks by source type to find the biggest repeated groups.
recommendations.trySuggestion: Remove repeated instructions, file trees, and logs; keep one canonical copy where needed.

recommendations.title: Exclude generated artifacts
recommendations.description: Keep generated directories and minified bundles out of the context window.
recommendations.whyItMatters: Generated files rarely help the model reason about source code and can consume significant budget.
recommendations.inspectSuggestion: Look at the generated-artifact blocks to confirm they are build output.
recommendations.trySuggestion: Add dist, build, coverage, and *.min.js to your file-collection ignore list.

recommendations.title: Deduplicate instructions
recommendations.description: Avoid resending the same project instructions every turn.
recommendations.whyItMatters: Repeated instructions inflate the context window without adding new information.
recommendations.inspectSuggestion: Open the Context Timeline and compare the project-instruction blocks across turns.
recommendations.trySuggestion: Move the instruction to a system prompt or configure your agent to send it only once.

recommendations.title: Compress or diff file trees
recommendations.description: Only send the file tree when it changes, or send a compact diff.
recommendations.whyItMatters: File trees are often large and stable; repeating the full tree wastes tokens.
recommendations.inspectSuggestion: Look at the file-tree blocks in the Context Timeline.
recommendations.trySuggestion: Send a diff of changed files, or omit the tree when no files were added or removed.

recommendations.title: Be cautious with lockfiles
recommendations.description: Include lockfiles only when dependency resolution is the active topic.
recommendations.whyItMatters: Lockfiles can be large and are usually irrelevant unless the task involves changing dependencies.
recommendations.inspectSuggestion: Check why the lockfile was included and whether dependency resolution is part of the task.
recommendations.trySuggestion: Remove the lockfile from the context and re-run to see if the response quality changes.

recommendations.title: Prioritize relevant files earlier
recommendations.description: Surface files likely to matter for the current task before noisy output accumulates.
recommendations.whyItMatters: Files that appear late may be crowded out by earlier tool output or repeated context.
recommendations.inspectSuggestion: Verify in the Context Timeline that the file is relevant and could have appeared earlier.
recommendations.trySuggestion: Adjust your agent's retrieval logic to rank task-relevant files higher in early turns.
